steps.expected: My Tickets page renders with ticket records
steps.observed: My Tickets page rendered with 6 owned-asset records

steps.expected: Each ticket shows purchase date, reference number, and activity context
steps.observed: Each ticket card shows: Ref # (e.g., ASSET-2026-00005, OA-20260326-4YU4KC), Purchased date (e.g., Mar 26, 2026), Delivered date where applicable, Quantity (e.g., 1 of 1 available), Total Paid

steps.expected: API returns records with purchasedAt, referenceNumber, deliveredAt fields
steps.observed: API returns 6 records each with purchasedAt timestamps, referenceNumber strings, and deliveredAt (null or timestamp)

evidence.network: GET /api/user/owned-assets -> 200 (6 records with timestamps and references)

steps.expected: My Listings page renders with listing records
steps.observed: My Listings page rendered with 6 listing records (5 seeded + 1 newly created)

steps.expected: Each listing shows listed date, reference number, and listing context
steps.observed: Each listing card shows: Ref # (e.g., LIST-2026-00001, LST-20260326-7HEW7F), Listed date (e.g., Mar 26, 2026), Sold date where applicable (e.g., for sold items), Cancelled date where applicable, Total Value

steps.expected: API returns records with listedAt, referenceNumber, soldAt, cancelledAt fields
steps.observed: API returns 6 records each with listedAt timestamps, referenceNumber strings, soldAt and cancelledAt (null or timestamp)

evidence.network: GET /api/user/listings -> 200 (6 records with timestamps and references)

assertions.title: Multiple statuses are represented in seeded account-center data

steps.expected: At least two distinct status badges across owned tickets
steps.observed: 5 distinct statuses found in owned assets: pending, confirmed, listed (Listed for Sale), delivered, sold

steps.expected: At least two distinct status badges across listings
steps.observed: 5 distinct statuses found in listings: draft, pending_sale (Pending Sale), active, sold, cancelled

steps.expected: API responses contain multiple distinct status values
steps.observed: Owned assets API: [pending, confirmed, listed, delivered, sold]. Listings API: [draft, pending_sale, active, sold, cancelled]

evidence.network: GET /api/user/owned-assets -> 200 (5 distinct statuses), GET /api/user/listings -> 200 (5 distinct statuses)

assertions.title: Empty states appear only when a dataset is truly empty

steps.action: Navigate to /my/tickets as seeded test user
steps.expected: Populated records shown, not empty state
steps.observed: My Tickets shows 6 populated owned-asset records with images, names, prices, statuses, references, and dates

steps.action: Navigate to /my/listings as seeded test user
steps.expected: Populated records shown, not empty state
steps.observed: My Listings shows 6 populated listing records with images, names, prices, statuses, references, and dates

steps.expected: Records ordered by newest activity first
steps.observed: Owned assets ordered newest first: Mar 26 (OA-20260326-4YU4KC) → Mar 25 (ASSET-2026-00005) → Mar 22 (ASSET-2026-00003) → Mar 18 (ASSET-2026-00002) → Mar 15 (ASSET-2026-00001) → Mar 12 (ASSET-2026-00004)

steps.expected: Records ordered by newest activity first
steps.observed: Listings ordered newest first: Mar 26 (LST-20260326-7HEW7F) → Mar 24 (LIST-2026-00005) → Mar 23 (LIST-2026-00004) → Mar 20 (LIST-2026-00001) → Mar 17 (LIST-2026-00002) → Mar 14 (LIST-2026-00003)

steps.expected: API returns in descending chronological order
steps.observed: Both /api/user/owned-assets and /api/user/listings return records sorted by purchasedAt/listedAt descending

evidence.network: API responses confirm descending chronological ordering for both owned-assets and listings

assertions.title: Guest browse to purchase reaches My Tickets

steps.action: Clear cookies to become guest, navigate to /products/product-stable-001
steps.expected: Product detail page loads as guest
steps.observed: Argentina Home Jersey 2026 product detail page loaded at $29.99 with Purchase and For Sale buttons

steps.expected: Redirect to login with callbackUrl preserving product page
steps.observed: Redirected to /login?callbackUrl=%2Fproducts%2Fproduct-stable-001%3Faction%3Dpurchase

steps.expected: Return to product detail page
steps.observed: Returned to /products/product-stable-001 with purchase flow auto-opened

steps.action: Complete purchase flow: quantity (1), summary ($29.99 total), confirm
steps.expected: Multi-step purchase flow completes
steps.observed: 3-step flow: quantity selection → order summary → confirm purchase, all completed successfully

steps.expected: Success state with link to My Tickets
steps.observed: Success modal: 'Purchase Complete! Your items have been added to My Tickets' with 'View My Tickets' button

steps.expected: Navigate to /my/tickets with new record visible
steps.observed: Navigated to /my/tickets. New 'Argentina Home Jersey 2026' record visible at top with Confirmed status, Ref # OA-20260326-4YU4KC, Purchased Mar 26, 2026, $29.99

evidence.network: POST purchase API -> success, GET /api/user/owned-assets -> 200 with new record

steps.expected: Event detail page loads
steps.observed: Jordan VS Argentina event detail page loaded at $89.99

steps.expected: Create Listing flow opens with quantity and ask price
steps.observed: Create Listing modal opened with item 'Jordan VS Argentina', Quantity 1, Ask Price $89.99

steps.action: Click Next to proceed to summary
steps.expected: Listing summary step shown
steps.observed: Listing Summary shown: Jordan VS Argentina, Qty 1, $89.99/unit, Total $89.99

steps.expected: Confirm listing step shown
steps.observed: Confirm Listing step: 'Are you ready to list your tickets for sale?' Total Value $89.99

steps.expected: Listing created successfully
steps.observed: Success modal: 'Listing Created! Your tickets are now listed for sale' with 'View My Listings' button

steps.expected: Navigate to /my/listings with new listing visible
steps.observed: Navigated to /my/listings. New 'Jordan VS Argentina' listing visible at top with Active status, Ref # LST-20260326-7HEW7F, Listed Mar 26, 2026, $89.99

evidence.network: POST listing API -> success, GET /api/user/listings -> 200 with new record

assertions.title: Canonical account-center routes are used consistently

steps.action: Complete purchase flow and verify redirect destination
steps.expected: Post-purchase lands on canonical /my/tickets route
steps.observed: After clicking 'View My Tickets' from purchase success, URL is http://localhost:3100/my/tickets (canonical route)

steps.action: Complete listing flow and verify redirect destination
steps.expected: Post-listing lands on canonical /my/listings route
steps.observed: After clicking 'View My Listings' from listing success, URL is http://localhost:3100/my/listings (canonical route)

steps.expected: app/my/tickets/page.tsx and app/my/listings/page.tsx exist as canonical routes
steps.observed: Both app/my/tickets/page.tsx and app/my/listings/page.tsx exist. Code in PurchaseFlowModal.tsx uses router.push('/my/tickets'), ListingEntryModal.tsx uses router.push('/my/listings')

steps.action: Verify no deprecated aliases are used
steps.expected: No deprecated or mismatched route aliases
steps.observed: Routes /my/tickets and /my/listings are the canonical implementations, no aliases detected

evidence.network: Post-purchase URL: /my/tickets, Post-listing URL: /my/listings. Code references: PurchaseFlowModal.tsx:84 router.push('/my/tickets'), ListingEntryModal.tsx:86 router.push('/my/listings')

frictions.description: After purchase confirmation, the success modal appears on the same product detail page URL rather than immediately redirecting to /my/tickets. User must click 'View My Tickets' button to navigate. Same pattern for listings.
frictions.resolution: This is by design - the success modal provides a clear CTA to navigate to the account surface. Both 'View My Tickets' and 'View My Listings' buttons correctly navigate to canonical routes.

frictions.description: Purchase flow auto-opens when returning from login with ?action=purchase parameter, which is smooth but the URL parameter persists in the address bar
frictions.resolution: This is expected behavior - the action parameter triggers the flow to auto-open after login callback return

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. My Tickets and My Listings show comprehensive activity context with timestamps, reference numbers, and stable ordering (newest first). Multiple statuses are well-represented across both surfaces (5 distinct statuses each). Seeded data populates both surfaces correctly with no empty states. Guest-to-purchase flow successfully creates a record visible in My Tickets. Create-listing flow successfully creates a record visible in My Listings. Both flows use canonical account-center routes (/my/tickets and /my/listings).
